categories.title: Inference & Serving
categories.blurb: Run and serve language models locally or at scale.

tools.name: Ollama
tools.description: A developer-friendly tool that downloads and runs local LLMs from the terminal with a built-in OpenAI-compatible API.

tools.name: llama.cpp
tools.description: A C/C++ inference engine that runs LLMs in the GGUF format on CPUs, Apple Silicon, and GPUs with low memory use.

tools.name: LocalAI
tools.description: A self-hosted server that exposes an OpenAI-compatible API for running text, vision, voice, and image models on local hardware.

tools.name: Jan
tools.description: An open-source desktop app that runs LLMs fully offline as a ChatGPT-style assistant on your own computer.

tools.name: llamafile
tools.description: A Mozilla project that packages a model and its runtime into one executable file you can copy and run on any OS.

tools.name: MLC LLM
tools.description: A machine-learning compiler that builds and runs LLMs across browsers, phones, and desktops using TVM-based code generation.

tools.name: KTransformers
tools.description: A framework for running large Mixture-of-Experts models locally by splitting work between CPU and GPU to fit limited VRAM.

tools.name: ggml
tools.description: The low-level C tensor library that powers llama.cpp and whisper.cpp for running models efficiently on local hardware.

tools.name: PowerInfer
tools.description: A local inference engine that speeds up LLM serving on consumer GPUs by keeping frequently used neurons on the GPU.

tools.name: Nexa SDK
tools.description: An on-device inference framework that runs LLMs and multimodal models across CPU, GPU, and NPU on desktop and mobile.

tools.name: ExLlamaV2
tools.description: A fast local inference library for running quantized LLMs in the EXL2 format on consumer NVIDIA GPUs.

tools.name: ExLlamaV3
tools.description: The next-generation ExLlama runtime that adds the EXL3 quantization format for faster local LLM inference on GPUs.

tools.name: vLLM
tools.description: A high-throughput LLM serving engine that uses PagedAttention and continuous batching to serve many requests at once.

tools.name: SGLang
tools.description: A serving framework for LLMs and multimodal models that boosts throughput by reusing shared prompt prefixes across requests.

tools.name: TensorRT-LLM
tools.description: NVIDIA's library that compiles LLMs into optimized engines for the fastest inference on its data-center GPUs.

tools.name: LMCache
tools.description: A KV-cache layer that stores and shares cached attention state across engines and requests to cut repeated computation.

tools.name: LMDeploy
tools.description: A toolkit for compressing and serving LLMs at scale, built around the C++ TurboMind engine with quantized KV cache support.

tools.name: NVIDIA Dynamo
tools.description: A distributed inference serving framework that splits prefill and decode across GPU fleets for large-scale model deployment.

tools.name: KServe
tools.description: A Kubernetes platform for deploying and autoscaling generative and predictive model inference across many frameworks.

tools.name: GPUStack
tools.description: An open-source GPU cluster manager that pools several machines into one OpenAI-compatible endpoint for serving models.

tools.name: AIBrix
tools.description: A set of cloud infrastructure components for serving vLLM at scale, including routing, autoscaling, and KV-cache management.

tools.name: llm-d
tools.description: A Kubernetes-native framework for running distributed LLM inference with disaggregated serving and smart request routing.

tools.name: BitNet
tools.description: Microsoft's inference framework for running extremely low-bit (1.58-bit) quantized LLMs efficiently on CPUs.

tools.name: bitsandbytes
tools.description: A library that adds 8-bit and 4-bit quantization to PyTorch models, widely used for loading and fine-tuning LLMs in low memory.

tools.name: AWQ
tools.description: The reference implementation of Activation-aware Weight Quantization for compressing LLMs to 4-bit with little accuracy loss.

tools.name: LLM Compressor
tools.description: A library for applying quantization and sparsity methods to LLMs to produce compressed models that run faster in vLLM.

tools.name: torchao
tools.description: PyTorch's native library for quantizing and applying low-bit data types to models for faster training and inference.

tools.name: Intel Neural Compressor
tools.description: An Intel toolkit for compressing models through quantization, pruning, and distillation across multiple deep-learning frameworks.

tools.name: GPTQModel
tools.description: A model quantization toolkit, successor to AutoGPTQ, that compresses LLMs with GPTQ, AWQ, and other methods across many chips.

tools.name: Optimum Quanto
tools.description: A Hugging Face PyTorch toolkit for quantizing model weights and activations to formats like int8, int4, and float8.

tools.name: HQQ
tools.description: A fast quantization method that compresses LLM weights to low bit widths without needing any calibration data.

categories.title: Agent Frameworks
categories.blurb: Build LLM agents that plan, call tools and work together.

tools.name: AutoGPT
tools.description: One of the earliest autonomous agent projects, now a platform for building and running agents from reusable blocks and workflows.

tools.name: LlamaIndex
tools.description: A data framework for connecting language models to your own documents and data sources, with built-in agent and retrieval (RAG) tooling.

tools.name: Agno
tools.description: A fast Python framework (formerly Phidata) for building agents with memory, tools, and multimodal inputs, plus a runtime for deploying them in production.

tools.name: DSPy
tools.description: A Stanford framework for programming language models with composable modules and automatic prompt optimization instead of hand-written prompts.

tools.name: LangGraph
tools.description: A library from the LangChain team for building stateful, graph-based agent workflows with explicit control over steps, memory, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.

tools.name: Semantic Kernel
tools.description: Microsoft's SDK for adding agents, plugins, and planning to apps across .NET, Python, and Java.

tools.name: smolagents
tools.description: A minimal agent library from Hugging Face where the model writes and runs Python code to call tools and complete tasks.

tools.name: Haystack
tools.description: An orchestration framework from deepset for building modular LLM pipelines and agents for search, RAG, and question answering.

tools.name: Mastra
tools.description: A TypeScript framework for building AI agents and applications with workflows, RAG, memory, and observability built in.

tools.name: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK)
tools.description: Google's code-first Python toolkit for building, testing, and deploying agents, with native support for multi-agent setups.

tools.name: Pydantic AI
tools.description: A type-safe Python agent framework from the Pydantic team that gives you validated, structured outputs from language models.

tools.name: Upsonic
tools.description: A Python framework for building autonomous agents with a focus on reliability, task verification, and tool use.

tools.name: Atomic Agents
tools.description: A lightweight Python framework that builds agents from small, reusable, schema-driven building blocks for predictable behavior.

tools.name: BeeAI Framework
tools.description: An open framework for building production agents in both Python and TypeScript, with a shared set of components across the two languages.

tools.name: MetaGPT
tools.description: A multi-agent framework that models a software company, assigning roles like product manager, architect, and engineer to generate code from a single prompt.

tools.name: AutoGen
tools.description: Microsoft Research's framework for building applications where multiple agents converse with each other and with tools to solve tasks.

tools.name: CrewAI
tools.description: A framework for assembling teams ('crews') of role-playing agents that divide tasks and collaborate to complete a goal.

tools.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
tools.description: OpenAI's lightweight Python SDK for building multi-agent workflows using explicit handoffs, tools, and guardrails.

tools.name: AgentScope
tools.description: A framework for building multi-agent applications with message passing, visual debugging tools, and distributed execution.

tools.name: OpenAI Swarm
tools.description: An educational, lightweight framework from OpenAI for experimenting with multi-agent coordination through handoffs and routines.

tools.name: CAMEL
tools.description: A research-oriented framework for studying and building communicating agents that cooperate through role-playing conversations.

tools.name: mcp-agent
tools.description: A framework for building agents that use Model Context Protocol servers as tools, with reusable multi-agent workflow patterns.

tools.name: AG2
tools.description: The community-driven fork of AutoGen for building and orchestrating multi-agent systems with conversational group chats and handoffs.

tools.name: Browser Use
tools.description: A Python library that lets agents control a real browser to read pages and complete tasks online from natural-language instructions.

tools.name: Open Interpreter
tools.description: A lightweight coding agent that runs code on your own computer to carry out tasks from natural-language requests.

tools.name: UI-TARS Desktop
tools.description: ByteDance's multimodal agent stack and desktop app that controls a computer's graphical interface using vision-language models.

tools.name: Stagehand
tools.description: A TypeScript browser-automation SDK from Browserbase that mixes natural-language actions with normal code for reliable web agents.

tools.name: Skyvern
tools.description: A tool that uses language models and computer vision to automate browser workflows without writing custom code for each website.

tools.name: Agent Zero
tools.description: A general-purpose personal agent framework that uses the computer, terminal, and code as its main tools to complete tasks.

tools.name: Cua
tools.description: Infrastructure for computer-use agents, providing sandboxes and SDKs that let agents control full desktops on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

tools.name: Nanobrowser
tools.description: An open-source Chrome extension that runs multi-agent web automation locally using your own model API key.

tools.name: Steel Browser
tools.description: An open-source browser API and sandbox that gives AI agents a managed browser to automate the web without handling infrastructure.

tools.name: LaVague
tools.description: A framework for building web agents that turn a goal into browser actions through a planning model and an action engine over Selenium or Playwright.

tools.name: Notte
tools.description: A framework for building web agents and deploying serverless web-automation functions on managed browser infrastructure.

tools.name: OpenAdapt
tools.description: A tool that records screen and input demonstrations and uses models to replay and automate desktop GUI workflows.

categories.title: LLM Orchestration
categories.blurb: Glue models, prompts and tools into real applications.

tools.name: LangChain
tools.description: A widely used Python and JavaScript framework for building LLM applications by composing models, prompts, tools, retrievers, and memory into chains.

tools.name: Letta
tools.description: A framework (formerly MemGPT) for building stateful agents with long-term memory that persists across sessions and conversations.

tools.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
tools.description: Microsoft's framework that merges AutoGen and Semantic Kernel ideas for building and running single and multi-agent systems in production.

tools.name: Strands Agents
tools.description: An AWS-backed, model-driven SDK for building agents in a few lines of code with built-in tool use and multi-agent support.

tools.name: ell
tools.description: A Python library that treats prompts as versioned functions, with tooling to track, visualize, and iterate on them as code.

tools.name: GEPA
tools.description: A reflective, evolutionary optimizer that improves prompts and other text components of a system using language-model feedback.

tools.name: LMQL
tools.description: A query language for LLMs that mixes Python control flow with prompts and constraints to script multi-step generation.

tools.name: AdalFlow
tools.description: A PyTorch-like library for building and auto-optimizing LLM pipelines, tuning prompts across the components of a task.

tools.name: TextGrad
tools.description: A library that optimizes prompts and other text variables using textual gradients, applying a backpropagation-like loop driven by LLM feedback.

tools.name: Mirascope
tools.description: A lightweight Python toolkit for writing LLM calls as typed functions with prompt templates, chaining, and a single interface across providers.

tools.name: Guidance
tools.description: A programming model that interleaves generation, prompting, and control logic to constrain output and enforce formats like JSON or regex patterns.

tools.name: Outlines
tools.description: A library for structured generation that constrains an LLM's token output to match a JSON schema, regex, or grammar so the result is always valid.

tools.name: Instructor
tools.description: A library that wraps an LLM client to return data validated against a schema, retrying automatically on invalid output, with SDKs in several languages.

tools.name: BAML
tools.description: A domain-specific language for defining LLM functions with typed schemas, parsing flexible model output into reliable structured data across many languages.

tools.name: Marvin
tools.description: A Python toolkit from Prefect for turning LLM calls into typed functions that extract, classify, and cast text into structured Python objects.

tools.name: LM Format Enforcer
tools.description: A library that enforces an output format such as JSON schema or regex by filtering the tokens an LLM is allowed to generate at each step.

tools.name: XGrammar
tools.description: A fast, portable engine for grammar-constrained decoding that guarantees LLM output follows a given structure, used inside many inference servers.

categories.title: RAG & Retrieval
categories.blurb: Feed models your own documents and data.

tools.name: Dify
tools.description: An open-source platform with a visual workflow builder for creating LLM and RAG applications without writing much code.

tools.name: RAGFlow
tools.description: A RAG engine built around deep document understanding that turns complex files into a grounded, citation-backed question-answering layer.

tools.name: LightRAG
tools.description: A graph-based RAG system that builds an entity-and-relationship knowledge graph for fast retrieval and easy incremental updates.

tools.name: GraphRAG
tools.description: Microsoft's graph-based RAG system that extracts a knowledge graph from documents to answer broad, multi-document questions.

tools.name: RAG-Anything
tools.description: A multimodal RAG framework that ingests text, images, tables, and equations from documents and builds a cross-modal knowledge graph for retrieval.

tools.name: txtai
tools.description: An all-in-one embeddings database for semantic search, RAG, and LLM workflows with built-in vector indexing and pipelines.

tools.name: PaperQA
tools.description: A RAG library focused on high-accuracy, citation-backed question answering over scientific papers and other documents.

tools.name: R2R
tools.description: A production-oriented RAG server with ingestion, hybrid search, knowledge graphs, user management, and observability exposed through a REST API.

tools.name: nano-graphrag
tools.description: A small, easy-to-modify reimplementation of GraphRAG for people who want a hackable graph-based retrieval codebase.

tools.name: Morphik
tools.description: A multimodal RAG stack that embeds full-page visuals alongside text so it can retrieve over diagrams, tables, and scanned PDFs.

tools.name: FlashRAG
tools.description: A modular research toolkit that implements and benchmarks many published RAG methods under one consistent interface for reproducible experiments.

tools.name: MinerU
tools.description: A document extraction tool that converts PDFs and Office files into clean Markdown or JSON, with strong handling of complex layouts and CJK content.

tools.name: Docling
tools.description: An IBM-originated document conversion pipeline that turns PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, and more into structured, LLM-ready Markdown or JSON.

tools.name: Marker
tools.description: A fast pipeline that converts PDFs and other documents to Markdown, JSON, or HTML while preserving tables, equations, and formatting.

tools.name: Unstructured
tools.description: A library for ingesting and preprocessing many document types into clean, chunked elements ready for RAG pipelines.

tools.name: Zerox
tools.description: A tool that OCRs documents by passing page images through a vision model to produce Markdown output for downstream use.

tools.name: PyMuPDF
tools.description: A high-performance Python library for reading, extracting, and rendering content from PDFs and other document formats.

tools.name: open-parse
tools.description: A Python library that visually parses and chunks documents, grouping related content so retrieval gets coherent passages.

tools.name: Chunkr
tools.description: A document ingestion service that runs layout analysis and OCR to turn PDFs and Office files into structured chunks for LLM use.

tools.name: semchunk
tools.description: A small library that splits text into semantically meaningful chunks for embedding and retrieval.

tools.name: Tantivy
tools.description: A fast full-text search engine library in Rust that provides BM25 keyword search for the lexical half of hybrid retrieval.

tools.name: FlagEmbedding
tools.description: BAAI's retrieval toolkit that provides the BGE embedding and cross-encoder reranker models used widely in RAG pipelines.

tools.name: Vespa
tools.description: A search and serving engine that natively combines vector, keyword (BM25), and structured search with built-in ranking for large-scale retrieval.

tools.name: RAGatouille
tools.description: A wrapper that makes it easy to train and use ColBERT late-interaction retrieval inside RAG pipelines.

tools.name: ColBERT
tools.description: The reference implementation of ColBERT late-interaction retrieval, which ranks passages using token-level vector matching.

tools.name: FastEmbed
tools.description: A lightweight library from Qdrant for generating dense, sparse, and late-interaction embeddings used in hybrid retrieval.

tools.name: BM25S
tools.description: A fast BM25 keyword-search implementation in Python built on NumPy and SciPy for the lexical side of hybrid search.

tools.name: rerankers
tools.description: A lightweight library that wraps many reranking and cross-encoder models behind one unified Python API.

tools.name: rank_bm25
tools.description: A simple Python implementation of BM25 and its variants for keyword-based document ranking.

tools.name: FlashRank
tools.description: A small, fast reranking library that runs compact cross-encoder models with ONNX for low-latency result reordering.

tools.name: Byaldi
tools.description: A simple library for late-interaction multimodal retrieval over document images using ColPali-style models.

tools.name: PyLate
tools.description: A library for training and serving ColBERT-style late-interaction retrieval and reranking models.

tools.name: RankLLM
tools.description: A toolkit for reranking retrieved passages with listwise LLM-based rankers and prompt-based methods.

tools.name: mxbai-rerank
tools.description: Mixedbread's open-source reranker model family with code for scoring and reordering retrieved passages.

categories.title: Vector Databases
categories.blurb: Store and search embeddings for semantic retrieval.

tools.name: Milvus
tools.description: A distributed vector database for storing and searching billions of embeddings at scale, with multiple index types and Kubernetes-native deployment.

tools.name: Qdrant
tools.description: A Rust-based vector search engine that stores embeddings with rich payload filtering for semantic search and recommendation systems.

tools.name: Chroma
tools.description: A developer-focused vector database designed for quickly building retrieval and RAG features with a simple Python and JavaScript API.

tools.name: pgvector
tools.description: A PostgreSQL extension that adds a vector data type and similarity search so you can store and query embeddings inside an existing Postgres database.

tools.name: Weaviate
tools.description: A vector database with built-in hybrid search and LLM-provider integrations for building semantic search and retrieval applications.

tools.name: Manticore Search
tools.description: A database and search engine for full-text, vector, and hybrid search that can act as a fast alternative to Elasticsearch.

tools.name: Orama
tools.description: A JavaScript search engine that runs in the browser, on the edge, or on a server and supports full-text, vector, and hybrid search.

tools.name: Marqo
tools.description: A vector search engine that generates and stores embeddings from text and images so applications can run semantic search without a separate embedding step.

tools.name: Infinity
tools.description: An AI-native database that offers hybrid search over dense vectors, sparse vectors, tensors, and full text from a single binary.

tools.name: pgvectorscale
tools.description: A PostgreSQL extension that complements pgvector with a DiskANN-based index to improve vector search performance and scale.

tools.name: pgvecto.rs
tools.description: A Rust-based PostgreSQL extension that adds scalable vector similarity search, now succeeded by VectorChord.

tools.name: Vald
tools.description: A Kubernetes-native distributed vector search engine for approximate nearest neighbor search across very large datasets.

tools.name: VectorChord
tools.description: A PostgreSQL extension for disk-friendly vector search and the successor to pgvecto.rs, compatible with pgvector data types and syntax.

tools.name: FAISS
tools.description: A library from Meta for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors, with both exact and approximate indexes.

tools.name: Annoy
tools.description: A C++ and Python library from Spotify for approximate nearest neighbor search using memory-mapped tree indexes.

tools.name: LanceDB
tools.description: An embedded vector database, similar in spirit to SQLite, that stores and queries embeddings on local disk or object storage for AI applications.

tools.name: Zvec
tools.description: An in-process vector database from Alibaba, built on the Proxima engine, for on-device and edge similarity search without a separate server.

tools.name: sqlite-vec
tools.description: A SQLite extension written in pure C that adds vector storage and similarity search, running anywhere SQLite runs including the browser via WASM.

tools.name: hnswlib
tools.description: A header-only C++ and Python library implementing the HNSW graph algorithm for fast approximate nearest neighbor search.

tools.name: USearch
tools.description: A small, fast single-file similarity search engine with bindings for many languages for nearest neighbor search over vectors.

tools.name: Voyager
tools.description: Spotify's HNSW-based nearest neighbor search library with production-ready Python and Java bindings, intended as a successor to Annoy.

tools.name: chromem-go
tools.description: An embeddable in-memory vector database for Go with a Chroma-like interface and optional persistence, for adding RAG features without a separate service.

tools.name: cuVS
tools.description: A RAPIDS library for vector search and clustering on the GPU, used directly or embedded inside other vector databases.

categories.title: Embeddings
categories.blurb: Turn text and data into vectors, and serve them fast.

tools.name: EmbeddingGemma (Gemma)
tools.description: Google DeepMind's Gemma repo, home to EmbeddingGemma, a 308M multilingual embedding model small enough to run on-device for RAG and semantic search.

tools.name: ColPali
tools.description: A vision-language embedding model that indexes whole document page images for retrieval, avoiding the need to parse PDFs into text first.

tools.name: Model2Vec
tools.description: A tool that distills any sentence transformer into a tiny, fast static embedding model (the Potion models) that runs on CPU without a neural network at inference.

tools.name: Instructor Embedding
tools.description: Instruction-tuned text embedding models that let you tailor embeddings to a task by prepending a natural-language instruction.

tools.name: Qwen3-Embedding
tools.description: Alibaba's open embedding and reranking models built on the Qwen3 base, available in 0.6B/4B/8B sizes and covering over 100 languages.

tools.name: LLM2Vec
tools.description: A method and toolkit that converts decoder-only large language models into strong text encoders for producing embeddings.

tools.name: ModernBERT
tools.description: A modernized BERT encoder with long context and faster inference, widely used as a backbone for fine-tuning new embedding and retrieval models.

tools.name: Nomic Contrastors
tools.description: Nomic's training code and recipes for the fully open nomic-embed long-context text and multimodal embedding models.

tools.name: GritLM
tools.description: Models and code for generative representational instruction tuning, where one model handles both text generation and embedding tasks.

tools.name: AnglE
tools.description: A library for training and running BERT- and LLM-based sentence embedding models using angle-optimized contrastive objectives.

tools.name: Snowflake Arctic Embed
tools.description: Snowflake's open family of retrieval-focused text embedding models, including multilingual v2.0 variants tuned for search and RAG.

tools.name: Granite Embedding
tools.description: IBM's open Granite embedding models, including compact English and 200+ language multilingual versions trained on enterprise-friendly licensed data.

tools.name: Stella
tools.description: The stella family of compact English text embedding models that rank highly on MTEB while keeping a small footprint.

tools.name: Sentence Transformers
tools.description: The standard Python framework for loading, training, and computing embeddings with sentence and reranking models.

tools.name: Text Embeddings Inference (TEI)
tools.description: Hugging Face's Rust-based server for deploying embedding, reranking, and sequence-classification models with high throughput on GPU or CPU.

tools.name: Infinity
tools.description: A high-throughput serving engine for text embeddings, rerankers, CLIP, and ColPali models, exposing an OpenAI-compatible API.

tools.name: Model2Vec-RS
tools.description: The official Rust implementation for running Model2Vec static embedding models with very low latency.

categories.title: Fine-Tuning & Training
categories.blurb: Adapt and train open models on your own data.

tools.name: LLaMA-Factory
tools.description: An end-to-end training suite with a web UI that covers pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and RLHF for hundreds of LLMs and multimodal models.

tools.name: Unsloth
tools.description: A library that speeds up LoRA and QLoRA fine-tuning while cutting memory use, aimed at training models on a single GPU.

tools.name: PEFT
tools.description: Hugging Face's library of parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods such as LoRA, DoRA, and prompt tuning that train small adapters instead of full models.

tools.name: ms-swift
tools.description: ModelScope's framework for fine-tuning and deploying 600+ LLMs and 300+ multimodal models, supporting PEFT and full-parameter SFT, DPO, and GRPO.

tools.name: Axolotl
tools.description: A config-driven tool for fine-tuning and post-training open LLMs that supports SFT, LoRA/QLoRA, DPO, GRPO, and multi-GPU training across many model families.

tools.name: torchtune
tools.description: A PyTorch-native library for fine-tuning and post-training LLMs with readable recipes and minimal abstractions.

tools.name: XTuner
tools.description: A toolkit for fine-tuning LLMs and vision-language models that supports LoRA/QLoRA and full-parameter training with low memory requirements.

tools.name: Open-Instruct
tools.description: AllenAI's codebase for instruction-tuning and post-training LLMs, including the recipes used to build the Tulu and OLMo model families.

tools.name: DeepSpeed
tools.description: A deep learning optimization library whose ZeRO memory partitioning and offloading let you train very large models across many GPUs.

tools.name: Megatron-LM
tools.description: NVIDIA's library for training large transformer models at scale using tensor, pipeline, and sequence parallelism.

tools.name: Accelerate
tools.description: A library that runs the same PyTorch training code across CPUs, multiple GPUs, and TPUs while handling mixed precision, FSDP, and DeepSpeed.

tools.name: Liger-Kernel
tools.description: A set of fused Triton kernels for common LLM layers that raises training throughput and lowers memory use as a drop-in replacement.

tools.name: TorchTitan
tools.description: A PyTorch-native platform for pre-training large models that combines FSDP, tensor, pipeline, and context parallelism in one codebase.

tools.name: Nanotron
tools.description: Hugging Face's minimal library for pre-training LLMs with 3D parallelism, designed to be readable and easy to modify.

tools.name: Open-R1
tools.description: An open reproduction of the DeepSeek-R1 reasoning pipeline, with scripts for GRPO training and reasoning-data generation.

tools.name: verl
tools.description: Volcano Engine's RL post-training framework (HybridFlow) for building GRPO, PPO, and other RL pipelines on top of FSDP, Megatron, and vLLM.

tools.name: TRL
tools.description: Hugging Face's post-training library with trainers for SFT, reward modeling, DPO, PPO, and GRPO to align language models with preferences.

tools.name: ART
tools.description: OpenPipe's Agent Reinforcement Trainer for post-training LLM agents on multi-step tasks using GRPO and rule- or judge-based rewards.

tools.name: OpenRLHF
tools.description: A Ray- and vLLM-based RLHF framework that scales PPO, GRPO, and REINFORCE++ training to models with 70B+ parameters.

tools.name: Alignment Handbook
tools.description: A set of recipes and scripts from Hugging Face showing how to run the full SFT-then-preference-alignment pipeline used to build aligned chat models.

tools.name: Verifiers
tools.description: A library for defining verifiable-reward environments and running reinforcement-learning fine-tuning of LLMs against those rewards.

tools.name: Safe-RLHF
tools.description: A modular RLHF framework from PKU for constrained value alignment that separates helpfulness and safety reward signals during training.

tools.name: NeMo-Aligner
tools.description: NVIDIA's scalable toolkit for model alignment with RLHF, DPO, SteerLM, and SPIN that can train the largest open LLMs across many GPUs.

categories.title: Evaluation & Testing
categories.blurb: Measure quality, catch regressions, benchmark models.

tools.name: MLflow
tools.description: An open-source ML and GenAI platform that, alongside experiment tracking, provides evaluation and monitoring tools for scoring and comparing LLM and agent outputs.

tools.name: promptfoo
tools.description: A developer-first CLI and library for testing and comparing prompts and models, with red-teaming probes for prompt injection, PII leaks, and other vulnerabilities.

tools.name: OpenAI Evals
tools.description: A framework and open registry for building and running evaluations of LLMs and LLM-based systems, including prompt chains and tool-using agents.

tools.name: DeepEval
tools.description: An open-source Python framework that tests LLM apps like unit tests, with 50+ metrics for RAG, agents, chatbots, and safety, and a Pytest integration for CI/CD.

tools.name: Ragas
tools.description: An evaluation toolkit focused on retrieval-augmented generation that scores answer faithfulness, context precision/recall, and relevancy, often without needing ground-truth labels.

tools.name: Arize Phoenix
tools.description: An open-source observability and evaluation tool for tracing LLM and agent behavior, running evals on traces, and troubleshooting issues in development and production.

tools.name: garak
tools.description: An LLM vulnerability scanner from NVIDIA with 100+ attack probes that test models for prompt injection, data leakage, jailbreaks, and other security weaknesses.

tools.name: Giskard
tools.description: An open-source library for testing and scanning LLM and ML models for issues like hallucination, bias, and toxicity, including multi-turn agent testing and a vulnerability scanner.

tools.name: PurpleLlama
tools.description: Meta's set of trust-and-safety tools and benchmarks, including CyberSecEval and Llama Guard, for evaluating and guarding the security of LLMs and AI systems.

tools.name: PyRIT
tools.description: Microsoft's Python Risk Identification Tool for red-teaming generative AI, using an attacker model to generate and refine adversarial and multi-turn prompts against a target.

tools.name: TruLens
tools.description: A library for tracing and evaluating LLM apps and agents using feedback functions, built on OpenTelemetry so each retrieval, generation, and tool call is captured as a span.

tools.name: Inspect AI
tools.description: A framework from the UK AI Safety Institute for writing and running LLM evaluations, with built-in support for prompts, tool use, multi-turn dialogue, and model-graded scoring.

tools.name: DeepTeam
tools.description: An open-source LLM red-teaming framework from the DeepEval team that runs adversarial attacks to test apps for safety, security, and compliance vulnerabilities.

tools.name: OpenEvals
tools.description: A LangChain package of ready-made evaluators for LLM apps, including LLM-as-judge scorers and helpers for assessing outputs against expected results.

tools.name: AgentEvals
tools.description: A LangChain package of ready-made evaluators for agent trajectories that judge the sequence of messages and tool calls against an expected path or via an LLM.

tools.name: LM Evaluation Harness
tools.description: EleutherAI's framework for few-shot evaluation of language models across 60+ academic benchmarks, used as the backend for many leaderboards.

tools.name: OpenCompass
tools.description: An LLM evaluation platform that runs models against 100+ datasets covering reasoning, knowledge, coding, and domain tasks, with leaderboards and multi-model support.

tools.name: SWE-bench
tools.description: A benchmark and containerized harness that tests whether language models can resolve real GitHub issues by generating patches that pass a repository's tests.

tools.name: simple-evals
tools.description: OpenAI's lightweight library for running standard zero-shot, chain-of-thought benchmarks like MMLU, MATH, and GPQA to measure model accuracy.

tools.name: lmms-eval
tools.description: An evaluation suite for large multimodal models that runs image, video, and audio benchmarks across many tasks with a unified, reproducible interface.

tools.name: AgentBench
tools.description: A benchmark that evaluates LLMs as agents across diverse interactive environments such as operating systems, databases, web browsing, and games.

tools.name: HELM
tools.description: Stanford CRFM's Holistic Evaluation of Language Models framework for reproducible, transparent benchmarking of foundation and multimodal models across many scenarios and metrics.

tools.name: LightEval
tools.description: Hugging Face's toolkit for evaluating LLMs on standard benchmarks across multiple inference backends, with custom task and metric definitions.

tools.name: Terminal-Bench
tools.description: A benchmark and execution harness that evaluates AI agents on real terminal tasks by connecting a model to a sandboxed shell environment.

tools.name: EvalPlus
tools.description: A rigorous benchmark suite for code-generation models that extends HumanEval and MBPP with many more test cases to catch edge-case failures.

tools.name: BigCode Evaluation Harness
tools.description: A framework for evaluating code-generation models across 15+ benchmarks such as HumanEval, MBPP, MultiPL-E, APPS, and DS-1000 with reproducible settings.

tools.name: OLMES
tools.description: Allen AI's standard for reproducible language-model evaluation that fixes prompt formats and settings so benchmark results can be compared fairly across models.

categories.title: Observability
categories.blurb: Trace, log and debug LLM apps in production.

tools.name: Langfuse
tools.description: A self-hostable platform for tracing LLM and agent calls, managing prompts, and running evaluations to debug and improve AI applications.

tools.name: Opik
tools.description: An open-source platform from Comet for tracing, evaluating, and monitoring LLM applications, RAG systems, and agent workflows with dashboards and LLM-as-judge metrics.

tools.name: Portkey AI Gateway
tools.description: An LLM gateway that routes calls to 100+ providers through one API and adds logging, tracing, caching, and fallbacks for production AI traffic.

tools.name: Evidently
tools.description: A monitoring and evaluation framework for ML and LLM systems that tracks output quality, drift, and test results over time with reports and dashboards.

tools.name: OpenLLMetry
tools.description: An OpenTelemetry-based SDK that auto-instruments LLM providers, vector databases, and frameworks so traces flow into any existing observability backend.

tools.name: Helicone
tools.description: A proxy-based observability platform that logs, monitors, and evaluates LLM API calls by routing requests through its endpoint with one line of code.

tools.name: AgentOps
tools.description: An SDK for monitoring AI agents that tracks LLM cost, session replays, and performance across frameworks like CrewAI, LangChain, and the OpenAI Agents SDK.

tools.name: Pydantic Logfire
tools.description: An observability platform from the Pydantic team that records LLM calls, agent runs, and tool invocations with tokens, cost, and latency attached.

tools.name: Agenta
tools.description: An LLM engineering platform that captures execution traces, links them to prompts and configs, and supports evaluation and monitoring of AI apps.

tools.name: Laminar
tools.description: A Rust-based tracing and evaluation platform built for debugging long-running AI agents with transcript views and SQL queries over trace data.

tools.name: OpenLIT
tools.description: An OpenTelemetry-native platform for LLM observability, GPU monitoring, evaluations, guardrails, and prompt management across many providers and agent frameworks.

tools.name: Langtrace
tools.description: An OpenTelemetry-based observability tool that captures real-time traces, metrics, and evaluations for LLMs, frameworks, and vector databases.

tools.name: Weave
tools.description: A toolkit from Weights & Biases that traces LLM calls and agent functions, scores evaluations, and visualizes results for AI application development.

tools.name: OpenInference
tools.description: A set of OpenTelemetry-based semantic conventions and instrumentation libraries that produce standardized traces for LLM and agent frameworks.

categories.title: Guardrails & Safety
categories.blurb: Validate outputs and protect against unsafe input.

tools.name: Guardrails AI
tools.description: A Python framework that wraps LLM calls with composable input/output validators (from the Guardrails Hub) to check structure, type, and safety risks before responses reach users.

tools.name: NeMo Guardrails
tools.description: NVIDIA's toolkit for adding programmable rails to LLM chat apps, using the Colang language to control dialog flow and block jailbreaks, prompt injection, and off-topic answers.

tools.name: LLM Guard
tools.description: A security toolkit from Protect AI with 35+ input and output scanners that sanitize prompts and responses for prompt injection, toxicity, PII leakage, and harmful content.

tools.name: Rebuff
tools.description: A prompt injection detector that combines heuristics, an LLM-based classifier, a vector store of past attacks, and canary tokens to catch attempts to subvert an LLM application.

tools.name: Vigil
tools.description: A Python library and REST API that scans LLM prompts and responses with YARA rules, transformer classifiers, and vector similarity to flag prompt injections and jailbreaks.

tools.name: Invariant Guardrails
tools.description: A rule-based guardrailing layer for LLM and MCP-powered agents that sits as a proxy and inspects tool calls and messages to block unsafe agent behavior.

tools.name: OpenAI Guardrails
tools.description: OpenAI's Python package that adds configurable safety and moderation checks as a drop-in wrapper around the OpenAI client for validating model inputs and outputs.

tools.name: Invariant Gateway
tools.description: An LLM proxy from Invariant Labs that sits between an agent and its model or MCP servers to observe, log, and debug what the agent is doing at runtime.

tools.name: Microsoft Presidio
tools.description: A framework for detecting, redacting, masking, and anonymizing personal data (PII) in text, images, and structured data using NER models, regex, and rule-based recognizers.

tools.name: GLiNER
tools.description: A small zero-shot named-entity recognition model that can extract arbitrary entity types from text and is widely used as a PII detection backend, including inside Presidio.

tools.name: Detoxify
tools.description: Pretrained transformer models from Unitary that score text for toxicity, insults, threats, and hate speech, often used to moderate LLM inputs and outputs.

tools.name: scrubadub
tools.description: A Python library that finds and removes personal information such as names, emails, and phone numbers from free text using detectors and optional spaCy or Stanford NER plugins.

tools.name: DataFog
tools.description: A lightweight Python SDK for PII detection and redaction with fast regex defaults and optional NLP, OCR, and Spark extras, aimed at scrubbing data before it reaches an LLM.

categories.title: Gateways & Routing
categories.blurb: One API in front of many model providers.

tools.name: LiteLLM
tools.description: A Python SDK and proxy server that gives one OpenAI-compatible API to 100+ LLM providers, with cost tracking, budgets, fallbacks, rate limiting, and an admin UI.

tools.name: Apache APISIX
tools.description: A cloud-native API gateway whose AI plugins add multi-provider LLM proxying, load balancing, retries and fallbacks, token-based rate limiting, and content moderation.

tools.name: Higress
tools.description: An AI-native API gateway built on Istio and Envoy that proxies and governs traffic to many LLM providers, with token rate limiting, caching, and MCP server hosting.

tools.name: Plano (formerly Arch Gateway)
tools.description: An Envoy-based proxy and data plane for agentic apps that handles prompt routing between agents, guardrails, unified access to LLMs, and observability.

tools.name: Bifrost
tools.description: A high-throughput LLM gateway written in Go that gives a single OpenAI-compatible API to many providers, with failover, load balancing, semantic caching, and very low overhead at high request rates.

tools.name: RouteLLM
tools.description: A framework from LMSYS for serving and evaluating LLM routers that sends easy queries to cheaper models and hard ones to stronger models to cut cost.

tools.name: vLLM Semantic Router
tools.description: An intelligent router that inspects each request and sends it to the most suitable model in a mixture-of-models setup across cloud, data center, and edge.

tools.name: AxonHub
tools.description: An open-source AI gateway that lets any SDK call 100+ LLMs with built-in failover, load balancing, cost control, and end-to-end request tracing.

tools.name: optillm
tools.description: An OpenAI-compatible optimizing inference proxy that applies reasoning techniques at request time to improve accuracy on math, coding, and logic tasks.

tools.name: Semantic Router (Aurelio Labs)
tools.description: A fast decision layer for LLMs and agents that uses embeddings to route requests to predefined paths or models without calling an extra LLM.

tools.name: LLMRouter
tools.description: An open-source library and CLI for cost-aware LLM routing that bundles many router algorithms and a plugin workflow for building custom routers.

tools.name: Envoy AI Gateway
tools.description: An open-source extension to Envoy Gateway that routes application traffic to generative AI services, handling authentication, rate limiting, and provider routing on Kubernetes.

tools.name: LLM Gateway (theopenco)
tools.description: An open-source gateway that routes, manages, and analyzes LLM requests across multiple providers through one OpenAI-compatible API with usage and cost tracking.

tools.name: Helicone AI Gateway
tools.description: A self-hosted gateway written in Rust that proxies requests to 100+ LLM providers through one OpenAI-compatible endpoint, with load balancing, caching, and built-in observability.

categories.title: App UIs & Frontends
categories.blurb: Ship chat and AI interfaces without building from scratch.

tools.name: Open WebUI
tools.description: A self-hosted web interface for chatting with local and API-based LLMs (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible) that adds RAG, web search, image generation, and multi-user access.

tools.name: Lobe Chat
tools.description: An open-source chat UI with a plugin and agent marketplace, multi-model support, voice, vision, and one-click self-hosting.

tools.name: AnythingLLM
tools.description: A desktop and self-hosted chat app focused on document Q&A and agents, letting you build private RAG workspaces over your own files and data sources.

tools.name: Cherry Studio
tools.description: A cross-platform desktop chat client that connects to many cloud and local model providers and ships with hundreds of preset assistants and MCP support.

tools.name: Chatbox
tools.description: A desktop and web chat client for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Ollama, and other models, with local message storage and multi-provider switching.

tools.name: LibreChat
tools.description: A self-hostable ChatGPT-style chat app that unifies many AI providers and adds agents, MCP support, artifacts, a code interpreter, and multi-user authentication.

tools.name: Vercel AI Chatbot
tools.description: An open-source Next.js starter template for building a full-featured AI chatbot with the Vercel AI SDK, auth, and multi-model routing.

tools.name: Hugging Face Chat UI
tools.description: The SvelteKit codebase behind HuggingChat for building a self-hosted chat interface over open models served from the Hugging Face Hub or a custom endpoint.

tools.name: big-AGI
tools.description: A self-hostable AI chat suite with multi-model side-by-side chats (Beam), personas, voice, image generation, and code execution.

tools.name: Deep Chat
tools.description: A framework-agnostic embeddable chat web component that you drop into any website and connect to OpenAI, Hugging Face, or your own backend, including in-browser models.

tools.name: CopilotKit
tools.description: A frontend framework for adding in-app AI copilots and agent-driven generative UI to React and other apps, and the maker of the AG-UI protocol.

tools.name: Vercel AI SDK
tools.description: A TypeScript toolkit for building AI apps and agents that includes streaming helpers and generative-UI features for rendering model output as React components.

tools.name: AG-UI Protocol
tools.description: An open protocol that standardizes how AI agents stream events and UI to frontend applications, with SDKs across multiple languages and frameworks.

tools.name: Tambo
tools.description: A generative UI SDK for React where you register components with Zod schemas and an agent picks the right one and streams its props to the user.

tools.name: assistant-ui
tools.description: A TypeScript/React library of composable chat primitives for building AI chat apps that can render tool calls and JSON as interactive React components.

tools.name: OpenUI (Thesys)
tools.description: An open standard and runtime for generative UI that uses a compact streaming language to let models emit interactive charts, forms, tables, and cards.

tools.name: Streamlit
tools.description: A Python framework that turns scripts into interactive data and ML web apps with simple widget calls and no frontend code.

tools.name: Gradio
tools.description: A Python library for quickly building shareable web demos and UIs for machine learning models, APIs, and arbitrary functions.

tools.name: Reflex
tools.description: A framework for building full-stack web apps entirely in Python, compiling component code to a React frontend and Python backend.

tools.name: Dash
tools.description: A Python framework from Plotly for building analytical web dashboards and data apps with interactive charts and no JavaScript required.

tools.name: marimo
tools.description: A reactive Python notebook stored as plain Python that can be run as a script or deployed as an interactive data app.

tools.name: NiceGUI
tools.description: A backend-first Python UI framework built on FastAPI and Vue for creating web interfaces, dashboards, and internal tools.

tools.name: Data Formulator
tools.description: A Microsoft Research tool that combines a UI with AI to help users create rich data visualizations through natural language and direct manipulation.

tools.name: Mesop
tools.description: A Python UI framework, started at Google, for rapidly building AI demos and internal web apps using composable components.

tools.name: Evidence
tools.description: A business-intelligence-as-code tool that builds interactive data reports and dashboards from SQL queries and Markdown files.

tools.name: Panel
tools.description: A Python dashboarding library in the HoloViz ecosystem for building data exploration apps that integrate deeply with PyData plotting tools.

tools.name: Solara
tools.description: A pure-Python, React-style framework for building data apps that run both inside Jupyter notebooks and as standalone web apps.

tools.name: Shiny for Python
tools.description: Posit's reactive Python framework for building interactive dashboards and data apps, a Python port of the R Shiny framework.

categories.title: Data & Scraping
categories.blurb: Collect, clean and label the data that feeds models.

tools.name: MarkItDown
tools.description: A Microsoft Python utility that converts many file types, including Office docs and PDFs, into Markdown for LLMs.

tools.name: Firecrawl
tools.description: A crawling service and API that converts whole websites into clean Markdown or structured JSON ready for LLMs.

tools.name: Crawl4AI
tools.description: A local-first Python web crawler that turns pages into clean Markdown for use in RAG and LLM pipelines.

tools.name: Scrapy
tools.description: A mature Python framework for writing fast spiders that crawl websites and extract structured data at scale.

tools.name: ScrapeGraphAI
tools.description: A Python library that uses LLMs and a graph pipeline to extract data from pages based on natural-language prompts.

tools.name: Colly
tools.description: A Go scraping framework for building fast crawlers with request handling, callbacks, and rate limiting.

tools.name: Crawlee
tools.description: A Node.js/TypeScript scraping library with proxy rotation and browser fingerprinting for building reliable crawlers.

tools.name: Katana
tools.description: A fast Go command-line crawler that discovers every URL, endpoint, and JavaScript file on a target site.

tools.name: Photon
tools.description: A fast Python web crawler that extracts URLs, emails, files, and other data while mapping a website.

tools.name: Jina Reader
tools.description: A service that converts any URL into clean LLM-friendly Markdown, handling JavaScript-rendered pages.

tools.name: Crawlee for Python
tools.description: The Python version of Crawlee for building crawlers that collect web data for AI, LLMs, and RAG.

tools.name: AutoScraper
tools.description: A Python library that learns scraping rules automatically from example data you want from a page.

tools.name: LLM Scraper
tools.description: A TypeScript library that extracts structured data from web pages using an LLM guided by a Zod schema.

tools.name: Trafilatura
tools.description: A Python tool that crawls pages and extracts the main text and metadata, stripping ads and boilerplate.

tools.name: Label Studio
tools.description: A flexible open-source labeling platform for images, text, audio, video, and time series with AI-assisted labeling.

tools.name: CVAT
tools.description: A computer-vision annotation tool for labeling images and video with bounding boxes, segmentation, and tracking.

tools.name: doccano
tools.description: An open-source text annotation tool for classification, sequence labeling, and sequence-to-sequence tasks.

tools.name: Argilla
tools.description: An open-source platform for annotating, reviewing, and curating datasets to train and fine-tune language models.

tools.name: SDV (Synthetic Data Vault)
tools.description: A Python library that learns patterns from real tabular data and generates realistic synthetic tables.

tools.name: distilabel
tools.description: A framework for building synthetic-data and AI-feedback pipelines that call any LLM to generate and judge datasets.

tools.name: NeMo Data Designer
tools.description: An NVIDIA library for orchestrating LLM-based synthetic-data pipelines with validation and quality scoring.

tools.name: Augmentoolkit
tools.description: A tool that turns raw documents into synthetic instruction and conversation datasets for fine-tuning models.

tools.name: Curator
tools.description: A Python library for generating and curating large synthetic datasets to train and post-train language models.

tools.name: ydata-synthetic
tools.description: A collection of generators for creating synthetic tabular and time-series data from real datasets.

tools.name: NeMo Curator
tools.description: An NVIDIA toolkit for cleaning, deduplicating, and curating large text datasets used to train LLMs.

tools.name: Synthetic Data Kit
tools.description: A Meta toolkit for generating and curating synthetic question-answer and reasoning data to fine-tune LLMs.

tools.name: CTGAN
tools.description: A deep-learning model that uses conditional GANs to generate high-fidelity synthetic tabular data.

tools.name: Synthetic Data Generator
tools.description: An Argilla tool that builds training datasets from natural-language descriptions using distilabel and LLMs.

categories.title: Multimodal
categories.blurb: Images, audio and vision — generation and understanding.

tools.name: Stable Diffusion web UI (AUTOMATIC1111)
tools.description: A browser interface for running Stable Diffusion image generation locally with extensions and fine-grained controls.

tools.name: ComfyUI
tools.description: A node-based visual editor for building and running image and video generation pipelines like Stable Diffusion and FLUX locally.

tools.name: Fooocus
tools.description: A simplified image generation app built on Stable Diffusion that hides technical settings for easy prompting.

tools.name: InvokeAI
tools.description: A self-hosted creative tool and canvas for generating and editing images with open diffusion models.

tools.name: Stability-AI generative-models
tools.description: Stability AI's official code for its Stable Diffusion family of image and video generation models.

tools.name: FLUX
tools.description: Black Forest Labs' open-weight diffusion models and inference code for generating and editing images from text prompts.

tools.name: Z-Image
tools.description: Alibaba Tongyi's 6B-parameter open image model that produces photorealistic images quickly on a single GPU.

tools.name: Qwen-Image
tools.description: Alibaba's open image generation and editing model that is good at rendering text inside images, including Chinese.

tools.name: HunyuanImage 2.1
tools.description: Tencent's open diffusion model for generating high-resolution 2K text-to-image results.

tools.name: Whisper
tools.description: OpenAI's speech recognition model that transcribes and translates audio across many languages.

tools.name: VibeVoice
tools.description: Microsoft's text-to-speech model for generating long, expressive multi-speaker audio like podcasts.

tools.name: Coqui TTS
tools.description: A library of text-to-speech models including the multilingual XTTS voice-cloning model.

tools.name: Fish Speech
tools.description: A multilingual text-to-speech and voice-cloning model trained on a large set of audio.

tools.name: Chatterbox
tools.description: Resemble AI's open text-to-speech and voice-cloning model that runs on a single GPU.

tools.name: IndexTTS
tools.description: An open text-to-speech and voice-cloning model with support for controllable, expressive speech.

tools.name: Dia
tools.description: A text-to-speech model that generates natural multi-speaker dialogue with nonverbal sounds like laughter.

tools.name: NVIDIA NeMo
tools.description: NVIDIA's framework for building speech recognition and text-to-speech models, including the Parakeet and Canary models.

tools.name: F5-TTS
tools.description: A flow-matching text-to-speech model that clones a voice from a short reference clip.

tools.name: Moshi
tools.description: Kyutai's real-time spoken dialogue model that listens and speaks at the same time for low-latency conversations.

tools.name: Moonshine
tools.description: A fast, small speech recognition model designed for real-time transcription on edge devices.

tools.name: Higgs Audio
tools.description: Boson AI's open audio model for expressive text-to-speech and voice cloning.

tools.name: Kokoro
tools.description: A small 82M-parameter text-to-speech model that runs fast on modest hardware including CPUs.

tools.name: PaddleOCR
tools.description: A toolkit for detecting and recognizing text in images across many languages, plus document parsing.

tools.name: Ultralytics YOLO
tools.description: A framework for training and running YOLO models for real-time object detection, segmentation, and tracking.

tools.name: Supervision
tools.description: A Python toolkit for processing, annotating, and visualizing detections and segmentations from many vision models.

tools.name: MMDetection
tools.description: An OpenMMLab toolbox with many object detection and instance segmentation algorithms for research and production.

tools.name: Segment Anything 2 (SAM 2)
tools.description: Meta's model for segmenting and tracking any object across images and video frames from clicks or boxes.

tools.name: Grounded-SAM
tools.description: A pipeline that combines Grounding DINO and Segment Anything to detect and segment objects from text prompts.

tools.name: DINOv3
tools.description: Meta's self-supervised vision backbone that produces general-purpose image features for many downstream tasks.

tools.name: Segment Anything 3 (SAM 3)
tools.description: Meta's segmentation model that detects, segments, and tracks objects in images and video from text or visual prompts.

tools.name: Grounding DINO
tools.description: An open-set object detector that finds objects in an image described by free-form text queries.

tools.name: Depth Anything V2
tools.description: A model that estimates depth from a single image for any scene.

tools.name: RF-DETR
tools.description: Roboflow's real-time transformer-based detector for object detection, segmentation, and classification.

tools.name: Sapiens
tools.description: Meta's family of vision models for human-centric tasks like pose estimation, body-part segmentation, and depth.

tools.name: SAHI
tools.description: A library that improves detection of small objects in large images by slicing them into tiles before inference.

tools.name: Roboflow Inference
tools.description: A server for deploying and running computer vision models like YOLO, SAM, and CLIP locally or at the edge.

categories.title: Workflow Automation
categories.blurb: Wire up AI workflows with low-code, visual builders.

tools.name: n8n
tools.description: A self-hostable workflow automation tool with a visual node editor that connects 400+ apps and APIs and adds native AI steps, letting technical teams build automations without writing most of the glue code.

tools.name: NocoDB
tools.description: An open-source tool that turns any SQL database into a no-code spreadsheet-style app, used as a self-hosted Airtable alternative.

tools.name: Huginn
tools.description: A self-hosted system of "agents" that watch websites, feeds, and events and take automated actions on your behalf, similar to a private IFTTT.

tools.name: Appsmith
tools.description: An open-source low-code platform for quickly building internal tools, admin panels, and dashboards from a visual editor plus custom JavaScript and data-source connections.

tools.name: ToolJet
tools.description: An open-source low-code platform with a drag-and-drop UI builder, built-in database, and JavaScript logic for building internal tools, dashboards, and workflows.

tools.name: Directus
tools.description: An open-source data platform that wraps any SQL database in an instant API and a no-code admin app for managing content and building back-office tools.

tools.name: Refine
tools.description: A React meta-framework for building data-heavy internal tools, admin panels, dashboards, and B2B apps, combining low-code generators with full code control.

tools.name: Budibase
tools.description: A low-code platform for building internal apps, forms, and automations on top of your data, aimed at teams that want quick self-hosted CRUD tools.

tools.name: Kestra
tools.description: An event-driven orchestration platform that defines data, AI, and infrastructure pipelines as declarative YAML and runs them through a web UI.

tools.name: Node-RED
tools.description: A flow-based, low-code tool for wiring together hardware devices, APIs, and online services by dragging and connecting nodes, widely used for IoT and event-driven automation.

tools.name: NocoBase
tools.description: A plugin-extensible, model-driven no-code/low-code platform for building internal business systems and tools that you run on your own infrastructure.

tools.name: Activepieces
tools.description: An open-source Zapier alternative where you build automations from reusable TypeScript "pieces", with support for AI agents and self-hosting.

tools.name: Teable
tools.description: A no-code, spreadsheet-style database app builder backed by Postgres that serves as a self-hostable Airtable alternative.

tools.name: Windmill
tools.description: A developer platform that turns scripts in several languages into APIs, scheduled jobs, visual workflows, and auto-generated UIs, blending code and low-code building.

tools.name: APITable
tools.description: An API-first low-code platform for building collaborative database apps, positioned as an open-source Airtable alternative.

tools.name: Automatisch
tools.description: A self-hosted, open-source alternative to Zapier for connecting services and building no-code workflow automations with no execution limits.

tools.name: StackStorm
tools.description: An event-driven automation platform for DevOps and SRE teams that runs rules and workflows to handle auto-remediation, incident response, and deployments.

tools.name: Baserow
tools.description: A self-hosted no-code database and app builder with a spreadsheet-like interface and built-in automations, offered as an Airtable alternative.

tools.name: Saltcorn
tools.description: An extensible open-source no-code builder for creating web and mobile database applications through configurable views, data types, and actions.

tools.name: Lowcoder
tools.description: An open-source low-code app builder for internal and customer-facing tools, continuing the discontinued Openblocks project as a Retool/Appsmith alternative.

categories.title: Agent Memory
categories.blurb: Give agents long-term memory and context.

tools.name: Mem0
tools.description: A memory layer that you add to existing LLM agents to extract, store, and recall user facts and preferences across sessions using vector, graph, and key-value backends.

tools.name: Graphiti
tools.description: A library that builds a temporal knowledge graph from an agent's conversations and data so facts can be tracked and queried as they change over time.

tools.name: Supermemory
tools.description: A memory and context engine that ingests information across tools and sessions and can run fully locally, acting as a second brain for AI applications.

tools.name: Cognee
tools.description: A graph-native memory engine that turns raw documents and conversations into a queryable knowledge graph for agents that need to build lasting knowledge.

tools.name: Memori
tools.description: An SQL-native memory engine that gives any LLM persistent, structured memory stored in standard PostgreSQL or MySQL databases instead of a vector store.

tools.name: MemU
tools.description: A memory layer for AI agents that organizes structured storage and intent capture to reduce the tokens needed to keep context across conversations.

tools.name: MemOS
tools.description: A self-evolving memory operating system for LLM agents that unifies storing, retrieving, and managing long-term memory with hybrid search and cross-task skill reuse.

tools.name: Zep
tools.description: A long-term memory service that asynchronously ingests, summarizes, and retrieves chat history for AI assistants without slowing down their responses.

tools.name: ReMe (MemoryScope)
tools.description: A memory management toolkit, formerly MemoryScope, that gives agents personalized long-term memory with retrieval, consolidation, and reflection operations.

tools.name: Memobase
tools.description: A user-profile-based long-term memory backend that tracks evolving facts about each user for chatbot and assistant applications.

tools.name: LangMem
tools.description: LangChain's memory SDK that helps agents store and retrieve summary, entity, and semantic memories, integrating directly with LangGraph workflows.

tools.name: A-MEM
tools.description: A research memory system that lets LLM agents dynamically organize and link their memories into an evolving note network inspired by the Zettelkasten method.

tools.name: Memoripy
tools.description: A Python library that adds short-term and long-term memory to AI applications, modeling concepts like memory decay and association for more context-aware recall.

tools.name: MemEngine
tools.description: A modular research library that implements many published agent memory models under one unified interface for building and comparing memory designs.

categories.title: MLOps & Infra
categories.blurb: Track experiments, deploy models, orchestrate compute.

tools.name: DVC
tools.description: A Git-like version control tool for datasets, models, and experiments that makes ML projects reproducible.

tools.name: Weights & Biases (wandb)
tools.description: An open-source client library for logging experiments, running hyperparameter sweeps, and versioning models and datasets.

tools.name: ClearML
tools.description: An open-source MLOps suite whose experiment manager auto-logs runs, metrics, and artifacts and ties them into data management and orchestration.

tools.name: Aim
tools.description: A self-hosted, open-source experiment tracker that logs and compares thousands of training runs through a fast web UI.

tools.name: Sacred
tools.description: A Python tool for configuring, organizing, logging, and reproducing computational research experiments.

tools.name: Polyaxon
tools.description: An open-source platform for tracking, scheduling, and managing large-scale deep learning experiments on Kubernetes.

tools.name: Trackio
tools.description: A lightweight open-source experiment tracker from Hugging Face that stores runs locally in SQLite as a drop-in alternative to wandb.

tools.name: OpenLLM
tools.description: A tool to run any open-source LLM as an OpenAI-compatible API endpoint locally or in the cloud.

tools.name: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server
tools.description: A multi-framework model server that runs TensorRT, PyTorch, ONNX, and other models with dynamic batching and concurrent execution.

tools.name: Xinference
tools.description: A serving platform that deploys language, embedding, and multimodal models through a unified API using vLLM, llama.cpp, or Transformers backends.

tools.name: BentoML
tools.description: A Python framework for packaging models and serving code into production inference APIs and containers.

tools.name: Aphrodite Engine
tools.description: A high-throughput LLM inference engine built on vLLM's PagedAttention with broad support for quantized model formats.

tools.name: Ray
tools.description: A distributed computing framework that scales Python and ML workloads for training, tuning, data processing, and serving.

tools.name: Prefect
tools.description: A Python-native workflow orchestration tool for scheduling, running, and monitoring data and ML pipelines.

tools.name: Kubeflow
tools.description: A Kubernetes toolkit that brings together pipelines, notebooks, and training operators for running ML workflows at scale.

tools.name: Dagster
tools.description: A data and ML pipeline orchestrator with a declarative asset model, built-in lineage, and observability.

tools.name: SkyPilot
tools.description: A framework that runs AI jobs across clouds and Kubernetes, automatically finding and provisioning the cheapest available GPUs.

tools.name: Metaflow
tools.description: A Python framework from Netflix for building and running data science and ML workflows that scale from laptop to cloud.

tools.name: Flyte
tools.description: A Kubernetes-native workflow engine for building reproducible, versioned ML and data pipelines.

tools.name: Volcano
tools.description: A Kubernetes batch scheduler that adds gang scheduling and queue management for AI and HPC training jobs.

tools.name: ZenML
tools.description: An extensible open-source MLOps framework for building portable, reproducible ML pipelines across different orchestration backends.

tools.name: Determined
tools.description: An open-source deep learning platform that manages distributed training, hyperparameter search, and GPU cluster scheduling.

tools.name: Kueue
tools.description: A Kubernetes-native job queueing system that manages quotas and admission for batch and ML training workloads.

tools.name: KubeRay
tools.description: A Kubernetes operator for deploying and scaling Ray clusters and jobs for distributed ML workloads.

tools.name: dstack
tools.description: An open-source control plane for provisioning and orchestrating GPU compute for ML across clouds, Kubernetes, and bare metal.

tools.name: Kubeflow Trainer
tools.description: A Kubernetes operator for running distributed model training and LLM fine-tuning jobs across frameworks like PyTorch and JAX.

tools.name: KAI Scheduler
tools.description: An open-source Kubernetes scheduler from NVIDIA that allocates GPUs to AI workloads with fair-share and topology-aware policies.
